metadata.title: Skill File Anatomy: Build Once, Invoke Every Semester
metadata.description_blurb: Teachers re-specify the same 280-word grading workflow every two weeks. A SKILL.md file stores it once and /grading-workflow runs it in 90 seconds instead of 18 minutes.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.narration_text: Teachers re-specify the same grading workflow every two weeks. Two hundred and eighty words, typed from memory, slightly different each time, because the last version is buried in a chat window they cannot find. A SKILL.md file stores the workflow once. Slash grading-workflow runs it in ninety seconds instead of eighteen minutes. That is the whole idea. This is Liam, in for Bear.

Claude reads the SKILL.md file, asks for the learning objectives and grade level, and produces a lesson plan saved to lesson-plans slash. The context cost of the skill is zero until you invoke it — it is not loaded at session start. First invocation: forty-five minutes to refine. Every invocation after: ninety seconds.
beats.new_visual_element: SKILL.md loads on invocation — 90 sec vs 18 min timer comparison

beats.narration_text: The SKILL.md anatomy. Frontmatter at the top names the skill and its trigger — whether Claude invokes it automatically or waits for an explicit slash command. The workflow block describes the steps. The Never section lists what the skill must not do. The Verification block says what a passing run looks like.
beats.new_visual_element: SKILL.md file anatomy — frontmatter / workflow / Never / Verification

beats.narration_text: The Never section is advisory — Claude tries to follow it, but it is probabilistic, not deterministic. If a rule must hold absolutely — for example, never generate a final grade — that enforcement belongs in a hook script, not a Never block. The skill defines intent. The hook enforces it.
beats.new_visual_element: Never section in SKILL.md — advisory vs deterministic callout

beats.narration_text: Babbage's insight: the unit of work is the named reusable procedure, not the per-session command. The skill is that unit. Build it once, invoke it every semester. The investment is forty-five minutes. The return is every invocation after.
beats.new_visual_element: Artifact view — SKILL.md template + invocation economics

beats.narration_text: Build your first skill. Paste this prompt — Claude will scaffold the SKILL.md from your workflow description.

props.command: Help me write a SKILL.md file for a workflow I run every two weeks. I will describe the steps; you draft the frontmatter, workflow block, Never section, and Verification block. Ask me the questions you need to fill each section.

beats.narration_text: If this saves your next eighteen-minute respec, subscribe. Liam, in for Bear.
